title: Media, Connectors, Transceivers, and Architectures · Operations Brief: Net Connectors
summary: Select and document a campus and data-center physical design under distance, bandwidth, environment, and redundancy constraints. Planned artifact: Add and verify the net connectors increment in a physical media and topology bill of design. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Identify SC, LC, ST, MPO, RJ11, RJ45, F-type, and BNC connectors and avoid incompatible media assumptions.
Compare 802.11, cellular, and satellite media by coverage, latency, capacity, interference, mobility, and cost.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Defend the Net Connectors model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Connectors applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Identify SC, LC, ST, MPO, RJ11, RJ45, F-type, and BNC connectors and avoid incompatible media assumptions.
